question_text: Let {1, 6, 5, 3} and {11, 7, 15, 4} be realizations of two independent random samples of size 4 from two separate populations having cumulative distribution functions 𝐹(⋅) and 𝐺(⋅), respectively, and probability density functions 𝑓(⋅) and 𝑔(⋅), respectively. To test 𝐻0: 𝐹(𝑡) = 𝐺(𝑡) for all 𝑡, against 𝐻1: 𝐹(𝑡) ≥𝐺(𝑡) with strict inequality for some 𝑡, let 𝑈𝑀𝑊 denote the Mann-Whitney 𝑈-test statistic. Let, under 𝐻0, 𝑃(𝑈𝑀𝑊> 12) ≤0.10, 𝑃(𝑈𝑀𝑊> 14) ≤0.05,⁡⁡⁡𝑃(𝑈𝑀𝑊> 15) ≤0.025⁡⁡ and 𝑃(𝑈𝑀𝑊> 16) ≤0.01. Then, based on the given data, which of the following statements is/are true?
